title: hieracium vulgatum subsp. irriguum
intro: Hieracium vulgatum subsp. irriguum, also known as the Rough Hawkweed, is a subspecies of perennial herb belonging to the dandelion-like Hawkweed genus. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving headaches, and promoting digestion. Hieracium vulgatum subsp. irriguum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used in traditional folk medicine, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
